title: rosa dumalis subsp. vosagiaca
intro: Rosa dumalis subsp. vosagiaca is a subspecies of the Rosa dumalis plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family, commonly known as the rose family. It is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, specifically in Iran and neighboring countries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a remedy for colds and fevers, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Rosa dumalis subsp. vosagiaca can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed for their health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
